plan_metadata.name: group  debug in teams channel local

steps.description: Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code, while viewing the "Preview README.md" document and Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit notifications.
Type 'Debug: Select and Start Debugging' into the VS Code Command Palette input field at the top of the window.
Press Enter to select "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" from the command palette dropdown in Visual Studio Code.
Type 'Debug in Teams (Chrome)' into the VS Code Run and Debug launch configuration dropdown at the top center of the window.
Press Enter to select "Debug in Teams (Chrome)" from the launch configuration dropdown in Visual Studio Code.
Click the browser tab's (+) button in Google Chrome at the top-left of the "Sign in to your account" tab to create new tab.
Click the "G" logo icon inside the Google Chrome address bar to activate the URL input field on the new tab page.
Type the URL 'https://localhost:53000/index.html#/config' into the Google Chrome address bar on a new tab window.
Press the Enter key in the Chrome browser’s address bar to navigate to "https://localhost:53000/index.html#/config" after highlighting the suggested URL.
Click the "Advanced" button on a Chrome browser privacy error page for a localhost connection (NET::ERR_CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID), located below the "Your connection is not private" warning message.
Click the "Proceed to localhost (unsafe)" link on the Chrome "Privacy error" page to bypass the certificate warning and continue to the localhost site.
Click the "Microsoft Teams Tab" browser tab in a Google Chrome window to activate it, as indicated by the red crosshair at coordinates (501, 20).
Click inside the "Email, phone, or Skype" input field on the Microsoft login page to activate and prepare it for text entry.
Enter ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}} into the email or username input field on the Microsoft Sign in page at login.microsoftonline.com in a Chrome browser.
Press the "Enter" key to submit the email address in the Microsoft Sign-in form and proceed to the next step.
Type ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}} into the 'Password' field on the Microsoft login page for user ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}.
Press the "Enter" key to submit the login password in the Microsoft sign-in page form after entering the "Enter password" field.
Click the "Yes" button on the "Stay signed in?" prompt within the Microsoft login interface to confirm staying signed in.
@assertion there's "Add" or "Open" blue button exist.
Click the "Add" or "Open" button on the app details popup within the Microsoft Teams interface.
Click inside the "Search for a channel or chat" input field in the toDoListlocal app success dialog within the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Type 'test-team' into the search input field under "Choose a place to use the app" in the "toDoListlocal" success dialog of the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the radio button next to "test-team" under the Channels section in the "Added successfully!" dialog after installing the toDoListlocal app in Microsoft Teams on the web.
Click the "Go" button at the bottom-right of the "Added successfully!" dialog in Microsoft Teams to confirm the channel selection for the toDoListlocal app.
Click the "Allow" button in the Microsoft Teams permission popup to enable local network access, within the Google Chrome browser interface.
Click the "Save" button in the "Tab Configuration" dialog within the "toDoListlocal" app in Microsoft Teams (web interface). The button is located at the bottom-right of the dialog.
@assertion there's "Welcome to To Do List App!" exist.
